name: Phil Robertson
title: Duck Commander & Evangelist
bio: American professional hunter, businessman, and reality television star who founded Duck Commander, a duck call company. Robertson was the star of A&E's Duck Dynasty, one of the most-watched reality shows in cable television history. A former college quarterback at Louisiana Tech (where he started ahead of Terry Bradshaw), he chose the outdoors over a potential NFL career. After years of hard drinking and a wild lifestyle that nearly destroyed his marriage, Robertson experienced a radical conversion to Christianity that transformed his life.
faith: Phil Robertson's conversion story is one of the most dramatic modern testimonies. After years of alcohol abuse, bar fights, and infidelity that led his wife Miss Kay to leave him, he was confronted by a local pastor and surrendered his life to Christ. He became an elder at White's Ferry Road Church of Christ in West Monroe, Louisiana, and dedicated the rest of his life to evangelism. He was famously suspended from Duck Dynasty in 2013 for unapologetically stating his biblical convictions in a GQ interview — and refused to back down. His testimony was depicted in the 2023 biographical film The Blind. He fathered a daughter from an affair in the 1970s (before his conversion), which he publicly acknowledged in 2020 — a fact that only underscores the depth of his transformation. Robertson spent his final years focused on his 'Unashamed' podcast and calling people to repentance and faith in Jesus Christ.
